rationale.R1.1: The draft's thesis (typed inter-agent communication), gap statement (free-form exchange as the central limitation), and contribution claim (first framework, 23% gain) are all present but could be sharpened; central to any substantive improvement.
rationale.R1.2: The argument chain — free-form causes ambiguity → hallucinations propagate → no precondition checking → StructAgents fixes this — needs to be logically tight; improvement work should check cross-sentence coherence and inferential gaps.
rationale.R1.4: The draft asserts StructAgents is 'the first multi-agent LLM framework to enforce typed inter-agent communication'; this strong novelty claim requires positioning against potential prior art and should be evaluated during improvement.
rationale.R1.6: The most natural reading of 'improve this draft' is prose quality — sentence clarity, word choice, and flow; load-bearing regardless of whether substantive or surface improvement is intended.
rationale.R1.7: Four references are cited (Li et al. 2023, Park et al. 2023, Shen et al. 2023, Yao et al. 2022); improving the draft includes verifying attribution accuracy, checking for missing related work, and assessing scholarly coverage.
